mechanism_narrative: A sustained downward trend in FEDFUNDS over the trailing 3 months lowers the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows and reduces borrowing costs for corporations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days regardless of the broader macro regime.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS 3-month change and forward 20d return remains below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window, or periods of falling FEDFUNDS show forward returns not significantly different from periods of rising FEDFUNDS (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window, it signals an inflection toward labor market deterioration that anticipates falling corporate earnings, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In triggered instances, SPX 20d forward returns are positive more than 55% of the time, or the mean triggered return is not significantly below the unconditional mean (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Decelerating payroll growth (PAYEMS 3-month annualized growth falling) combined with a still-elevated FEDFUNDS level indicates the Fed is over-tight relative to labor conditions, raising recession odds and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter (60 trading days). Neither series alone captures this risk; the joint state of slowing employment and restrictive policy is required.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with labor slowdown
failure_condition: Joint-trigger instances produce positive 60d forward returns in more than 50% of cases, or the interaction term's marginal contribution to Rank IC over the single-series baselines is statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion regime, sustained month-over-month growth in RSAFS reflects resilient consumer demand that supports corporate revenue beats, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days; the same signal is weaker or reversed in early-cycle recovery when retail sales growth reflects mean-reversion from a low base rather than genuine strength.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS MoM growth > 0.3% for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: The positive relationship between RSAFS growth triggers and forward 20d returns fails to hold specifically in late-cycle windows (identified via UNRATE near cycle lows), i.e., Rank IC in that regime subsample is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rising trend in INDPRO over the trailing month signals expanding industrial output and corporate production capacity utilization, which supports near-term equity momentum and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO MoM change and forward 5d return is below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window, or the sign of the correlation flips across more than two consecutive rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in HOUST exceeding 10% year-over-year signals contracting housing demand and forward-looking weakness in interest-rate-sensitive sectors, foreshadowing a broader economic slowdown that produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter (60 trading days).
failure_condition: Triggered instances of HOUST YoY decline > 10% produce positive 60d forward returns in more than 55% of cases, or mean triggered return does not differ significantly from baseline (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS exceeds the level implied by current UNRATE relative to its historical relationship (i.e., policy rate is restrictive relative to labor slack), the combination signals an elevated risk of policy-induced recession, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. Neither the rate level nor the unemployment level alone predicts this outcome; it is the mismatch between them that matters.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS - (natural rate proxy implied by UNRATE trend) > 1.5pp
failure_condition: The interaction signal's Rank IC is not meaningfully higher than the better of the two single-series Rank ICs (difference < 0.02) over a 3-year rolling window, indicating no genuine interaction effect.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle (FEDFUNDS rising over trailing 6 months), any deceleration in PAYEMS month-over-month growth is interpreted by markets as confirmation that tightening is working, triggering rapid negative repricing of equities over the next 5 trading days; in an easing or neutral regime the same payroll deceleration has no reliable directional effect.
failure_condition: During identified tightening-cycle windows, PAYEMS deceleration months show forward 5d returns not significantly below non-deceleration months (p > 0.05), or the effect appears with equal strength during easing regimes.
